title: End of Evangelion Review Series ONGOING
display_title: End of Evangelion Review Series ONGOING
wikitext:
[]: End of Evangelion Review Series (ONGOING)             Quote Post by FrDougal9000 » Sun May 26, 2013 4:41 pm Hey there everyone; a week or two ago, I posted that I was planning on doing a long in-depth review of the End of Evangelion and asked you guys as to which version I should review/feature footage (Here's the link to said post: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/14414/End-of-Evangelion-Japanese-or-English/) Anyways, thank you very much for the replies from everyone who answered my question. But what I'm really here for is to post each part as they're released (Which won't be within a week or so but rather a month or two). So, without further ado, here's End of Evangelion Review Part 1.11: Anno Did (Not) Troll Everyone! https://vimeo.com/67007468 EDIT: There's now a version on YT that addresses some but not all of the improvements suggested to me. Anyways, hope you enjoy this as well: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=J35e-p1rCzk Tell me what you think of it and give me any advice on improvements on my speech, editing etc. At the very least, I hope you enjoy the first in what I plan in being a 5-part review, each covering a different topic. Have fun, everyone!

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sun May 26, 2013 5:27 pm Hopefully the following isn't too mean, it's just some things that popped to mind as I watched... 1. Wouldn't it be easier to label this review as something different, so you wouldn't have to start it up with an intro that has nothing but video games? 2. Any reason why the Spike Spencer scene ran a little long? Probably just one or two lines would of done it fine... 3. Mentioning JesuOtaku's review and Benett's review will probably going to be lost to some people, since you didn't really point out some of the points their reviews brought up. 4. Half of the review was pretty much your explanation as to why you're doing this review in the first place. I'm curious: why did you decide to include it as a first part, instead of making that half its' own video? 5. Was the text comments a reference to Jesu's text? 6. I'm sure you're aware of the mic problems, so I'm not going to be critical about it. Other that those quibbles, I liked it. I appreciated on how somber the later half was, and I'm curious on where you're going with it. It would of been nice to hear some of the comments GAINAX had about episode 25/26, but that would of probably bloated the video out more. So, yeah, keep it up.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun May 26, 2013 6:09 pm Making a point of debunking the death threats thing. I'm sold.

[]:             Quote Post by FrDougal9000 » Mon May 27, 2013 11:30 am C.A.P. wrote: 1. Wouldn't it be easier to label this review as something different, so you wouldn't have to start it up with an intro that has nothing but video games?

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Mon May 27, 2013 11:32 am ^No, it did. And it's fine, I have a bad habit of being too vague at times.

[]:             Quote Post by Caliburn » Tue May 28, 2013 1:55 am Things I liked: 1. You speak at a good pace and are easy to understand. (This is not to be taken for granted with YouTube videos.) 2. While you're preaching to the choir in my case, it is always nice to see the rumor that Anno was trolling everyone with EoE debunked. I particularly liked your use of the animatic for Ep. 25. 3. There's good content here, like why EoTV ended as it did and how EoE came to be. Things I didn't like: 4. It's unclear who this video is for. Is it a review for people who haven't watched EoE yet? Is it a commentary for people who have but aren't sure what to think about it? 5. Why so much preamble? "And before we begin, allow me to explain..." could practically be the tagline for this entire video. I get that you are trying to provide context, but I feel you have much more context than is necessary. 6. The current video is 11 minutes. Given that it is part 1 of 5, I will extrapolate that the whole thing might be about 55 minutes. Why would I want to watch a 55 minute review for a film that is only 87 minutes long? 7. Examples of things to cut: Acknowledging the movie has been reviewed by others in the past (So what? Your opinion is valid too.), explaining there won't be many jokes (Why do there need to be?) 8. It's virtually impossible for me to take seriously any video that uses the English dub, but I fully admit that's a personal bias. :) Still, you may want to keep that in mind in terms of who your audience is.

[]:             Quote Post by FrDougal9000 » Tue May 28, 2013 5:23 pm Caliburn wrote: Things I didn't like: 4. It's unclear who this video is for. Is it a review for people who haven't watched EoE yet? Is it a commentary for people who have but aren't sure what to think about it?

[]:             Quote Post by Caliburn » Wed May 29, 2013 2:38 am 1.5 - 2 hrs? Wow, OK, I see now the scope of what you have in mind. In my case, I suspect I already know most of the relevant Eva background information from reading many interviews and articles over the years, so I don't think a commentary of that length is for me, but presumably others who maybe just watched EoE but are still processing what they just watched will appreciate having your videos to turn to. Have fun and good luck!

[]:             Quote Post by FrDougal9000 » Wed May 29, 2013 12:47 pm Alright, just a question I wanted to ask to anyone who cares about this thread; I'm thinking of names for the next four parts and I thought I'd ask for your help on this. Tell me if these names are fine, or if you have any better ideas. All help is welcomed and appreciated. End of Eva Part 2.22: Love isn't Destructive, War is (Episode 25') End of Eva Part 3.33: The Trials of a Dub Fan (English Mangle Dub Lesson) End of Eva Part 4.44: The Only Neat Thing To Do... (Episode 26') End of Eva ONE MORE FINAL: I need you (to keep watching) (Final opinion+evaluation)
